articles.title: US Congress Passes Its Biggest Housing Affordability Law in Decades
articles.summary: The United States government has taken a major step to make homes more affordable for ordinary people. Both houses of Congress agreed on a wide-ranging new law that aims to lower the cost of housing by increasing the number of homes being built across the country. The bill was supported by politicians from different parties, which made it unusual in today's divided political climate. Once signed by the president, the law could help millions of families who have struggled to afford a place to live. Experts say more homes being built means prices should gradually come down for buyers and renters alike.

lv1: What does the new US law aim to do about housing? (美國新法律旨在對住屋做什麼？)
Did politicians from only one party support this housing law? (只有一個黨的政治人物支持這項住屋法律嗎？)
lv2: Why might building more homes help make housing cheaper for families? (為什麼增加建屋數量可能有助降低家庭的住屋費用？)
Why is it significant that politicians from different parties agreed on this bill? (來自不同政黨的政治人物就這項法案達成共識，為什麼重要？)
lv3: Some people argue that simply building more homes does not solve housing unaffordability — do you agree? What other steps might governments take? (有人認為單靠興建更多房屋並不能解決住屋負擔問題，你同意嗎？政府還可以採取哪些措施？)
Should governments have the power to control how much landlords charge for rent? What are the benefits and drawbacks of such a policy? (政府是否應有權管制業主的租金水平？這種政策有什麼好處和壞處？)

articles.title: Hong Kong Releases New Blueprint to Bring Digital Learning into Every School
articles.summary: Hong Kong's Curriculum Development Council has published a brand-new plan to strengthen digital education in all primary and secondary schools across the city. The Blueprint for Digital Education Development sets out how schools should teach students to use technology wisely and safely, preparing young people for a world that increasingly depends on digital skills. The plan covers areas such as coding, artificial intelligence awareness, and responsible use of the internet. Education officials hope that by following this roadmap, every student in Hong Kong — regardless of their school — will have equal opportunities to develop the digital skills they need for future study and work.

lv1: Which organisation in Hong Kong released the new digital education plan? (香港哪個機構發布了新的數碼教育計劃？)
Does the new blueprint apply to primary schools, secondary schools, or both? (新藍圖適用於小學、中學，還是兩者都包括？)
lv2: Why do you think it is important for students to learn about artificial intelligence and coding at school? (你認為學生在學校學習人工智能和編程為什麼重要？)
How might this digital education blueprint help students who go to schools with fewer resources? (這份數碼教育藍圖如何幫助資源較少的學校的學生？)
lv3: Some critics worry that too much focus on technology in schools could reduce time for arts, sports, and social skills. Do you think digital education should be prioritised over other subjects? (有批評者擔心學校過度重視科技，可能減少藝術、體育和社交技能的學習時間。你認為數碼教育應比其他科目優先嗎？)

If digital skills become essential for most jobs, does that mean students who cannot afford devices at home are at an unfair disadvantage? What should society do about this? (如果數碼技能成為大多數工作的必備條件，那麼家中買不起電子設備的學生是否處於不公平的劣勢？社會應如何應對？)

articles.title: 港公務員加薪2%　中低層加幅創10年新低
articles.summary: 香港政府宣布公務員今年整體加薪2%，惟工會批評有關加幅是近十年最低水平，反映政府未有充分考慮基層公務員的生活負擔。工會指出，若當局日後引入不確定的計算指標，中低層職系的薪酬增長空間將進一步受壓。政府表示，制訂薪酬調整時已綜合考慮財政狀況及公眾利益，務求取得平衡。此事引起社會廣泛討論，不少市民關注公務員待遇與整體經濟環境的關係。

lv1: 今年香港公務員的加薪幅度是多少？
工會認為今次加薪幅度是近多少年來最低？
lv2: 為什麼工會對今次公務員加薪幅度感到不滿？
政府在決定公務員薪酬調整時，需要考慮哪些因素？
lv3: 你認為政府在制訂公務員薪酬時，應該優先考慮財政預算還是員工生活質素？理由是什麼？
如果公務員薪酬長期偏低，可能對香港的公共服務帶來什麼影響？社會應如何取得平衡？

articles.title: 山西失明男孩以721高分考入大學　將攻讀電腦科學與中醫雙學位
articles.summary: 2013年，年僅6歲的山西男孩郭斌遭人傷害，導致雙眼永久失明。然而，他並沒有因此放棄學習。在最近舉行的全國殘疾人特殊教育高考中，郭斌以721分（滿分800分）的優異成績，位列全國同專業第一名，成功獲長春大學錄取，將同時攻讀計算機科學與技術及中醫雙學位。他的故事感動無數人，充分展示了面對逆境仍能堅持不懈的精神，激勵了眾多年輕學子。

lv1: 郭斌在特殊教育高考中考獲多少分？
郭斌將會在哪所大學就讀？
lv2: 郭斌在失去視力後，仍能取得如此優異的成績，你認為這需要克服哪些困難？
社會應如何為有視障的學生提供更好的學習支援？
lv3: 郭斌的故事說明個人意志與社會支援哪個更重要？你認為兩者之間應如何配合，才能讓殘疾人士充分發揮潛能？
現時社會對殘疾人士的教育和就業機會是否足夠公平？若不足，政府和學校可以採取什麼具體措施加以改善？
